title: How I landed a software engineering internship at Citadel
body: My friend  just did Citadel first interview. It started of with C++ STL knowledge (he struggled with this) so He would advise anyone going into this interview to study this thoroughly. Then he moved onto a standard LC medium which he found to be the easiest part of the interview. The interviewer was a really nice guy, I think it was obvious tho that he didn’t have a great understanding of the intricate details of C++ so to anyone doing this interview in the future he would definitely study up on C++ and how it works. I hope you are able to make a more deep video about what type of questions they ask regarding  c++ language
author: @johnbarnes7485

title: How I landed a software engineering internship at Citadel
body: To say you have to be knowledgable about software engineering to a certain extent is a heavy understatement. You have to be good to get into citadel. There is skill and knowledge check at these interviews and to even knock on the door, you must have good projects and a degree (not entirely essential apparently). After all that, then you can focus on your “real person”. Don’t waste time. All you talked about was basically just soft skills.

title: Is it good for HOME DEFENSE? - the Citadel Boss 25 semiauto 12 Gauge
body: It's nice to see reviews of this gun by actual owners, instead of dudes on Reddit who don't own one and have never shot one taking a dump on it because it comes from Turkey.
author: @timstich1052

title: Is it good for HOME DEFENSE? - the Citadel Boss 25 semiauto 12 Gauge
body: I bought mine a couple months ago, been saving up for ammo, FOR ME it ran perfectly across the three main shell types (bird, buck and slug). i chose the 1600+fps slugs over everything because not only does it cycle the best but it feels like you are shooting normal buckshot due to the spring. ran all the shells i could through it and it ran perfectly with no failures or catch up's, i didn't even have to adjust the sights, it was already zeroed at 25 meters (27 yards) which i did find odd but it worked fine for what i need it for. HIGHLY recommended shotgun no doubt, amazing for the price!
author: @Milk_Man_1-1

title: Is it good for HOME DEFENSE? - the Citadel Boss 25 semiauto 12 Gauge
body: I purchased this gun and after the break-in rounds around 200, This gun is Ace! I just bought 10-round magazines that i need to break in and a 20 round barrel mag This gun is fun, and Its a gun I have right next to my other top shotguns!
author: @dperk001

body: Using it for self defense there is absolutely nobody that is going to hang around after they hear the first thunder of a 12 gauge going off most definitely going to add to my gun cabinet
author: @austingoleman9431

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body: Picked one up today for 320 bucks. It’s interesting to see how they’ve fine tuned it from this video from 3 years back. Has a much better trigger now. Great buy!
author: @Erichmustaine88

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body:
I've had mine for over a year now..First and foremost before you load that first round..totally disassemble the shotgun and clean all moving parts of the heavy shipping/cutting oil and lube it with fine gun oil..Second look at what loads you plan to shoot and make sure the piston weight ( you get two different weights with the rifle) is the correct weight for the loads your shooting ..Doing these two inportant steps will make this combat style shotgun cycle correctly and reliable...I've shot deer slugs to 00 buck in thousands of rounds without failure so where it counts the Boss 25 is built well enough, where as my buddy's Rockisland VR80 has broken a firing pin and charging handle retaining bearing in less than 6 months of weekend use..
Slings, optics , foregrip folding handles all fit but since this is a heavy cycling weapon buy quality built stuff to adorn your boss 25, not China made Amazon junk ...
author: @adamtara8185

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body: guys at the range firing a 556... I shot the Boss with Federal power shox Slugs.10 round magazine.. They were in Aaaaaaaaaa 😩...Dude was like " Man wtf is that?? 🤣..  I got that Big Boy💯
author: @Gwalt.X.W

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body:
Absolutely awesome shotgun! Even my father is very much interested in one, that's saying alot. Even some of the "old timers" at the local shop speak highly of this model which is very good to hear. Everyone seems to agree 1300fps and above is the sweet spot, unless well broken in I assume. For us highly respectful, responsible and law abiding citizens this is a whole lotta fun, perhaps the most we can have outside of the bedroom 😎

Have you had a chance to run it longer? I know it's not yours but still worth the question. Still waiting on mine from our local shop. Zero videos on the included chokes, the difference between the hotter/slower load gas blocks
author: @johnserrano9689

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body: I ordered one about a month ago. I picked it up and having never shot a semi auto shotgun this thing is amazing!!! I absolutely love it. I completely stripped it down and cleaned it out of the box, put roughly 300 rounds through it running 1600fps ammo slugs, 00 buck etc. Have has 1 hiccup that's it. This thing is badass in my opinion. 50 yards with a slug using hard sights and now a red dot can't wait to try out red dot.
author: @Jason2nd4life

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body: I am definitely looking to get one of these!  I already have 2 pump-action 12-gauge shotguns - A Mossberg 88 smooth bore and a Mossberg 500 slug gun… a Citadel Boss 25 will be a great addition to the collection.
author: @MoonGhost1301

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body: You can remove the cheek riser... easily. Cleaner look. Added a QR mount to the hole in the stock too...  Love mine.
author: @zootcapri

title: Citadel Boss 25 Shotgun First Look
body: When you’re only spending a few hundred dollars on a tactical shotgun just know it’s gonna take some break in time with high velocity ammo but afterwards it should be fine. I just bought a GF-25, basically the same gun. All these  Turkish guns are made alike and use the same mags. I’ve had the MKA 1919 and G-12 for several years and they run fine. Great guns for the money, just gotta break them in.
